meta.description: An identifiable single robbery, assault, or property/person crime incident. Includes theft, mugging, carjacking, and general security events involving property or personal crime. Each incident is a specific occurrence distinguishable by its location and date — not a crime trend, security overview, or statistics report. Extracted from news articles and social media.

example.description: Armed carjacking reported on Blvd. Lopez Mateos, where two suspects stole a vehicle at gunpoint.
example.context: Authorities report an increase in carjackings in the area over the past month.

date_range.mention: la noche del lunes 15 de septiembre

event_type.description: Type of crime from the catalogue. Choose the single most specific category that matches.

status.description: Status of the incident as reported.

name.description: Name or identifier for the incident, if any. Usually null for common crimes.

description.description: Brief free-text description of the incident.

context.description: Contextual information mentioned in the article.

relevance.description: Relevance within the article: 1 = main topic, 2 = secondary, 3 = implicit.

date_range.description: Date or date range when the incident occurred. Extract the start datetime and, if present, the end datetime.

victim_count.description: Number of victims, with original text mention and parsed count.

stolen_items.description: Description of stolen items or property, if mentioned.

arrests_made.description: Whether arrests were reported in connection with the incident.

responding_authority.description: Law enforcement or authority that responded, if mentioned.
